(born 16 May 1973) is a Kenyan long-distance and marathon runner. She won the 2000 [Venice Marathon](/wiki/Venice_Marathon "Venice Marathon"), 2003 [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid_Marathon "Madrid Marathon"), [Venice](/wiki/Venice_Marathon "Venice Marathon") and [Dublin Marathons](/wiki/Dublin_Marathon "Dublin Marathon"), 2007 [Luxembourg Marathon](/wiki/Luxembourg_Marathon "Luxembourg Marathon"), and 2010 [Tallinn Marathon](/wiki/Tallinn_Marathon "Tallinn Marathon").

[Historique Il était une fois …](http://www.marseille-cassis.com/la-course/historique/) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20090416172009/http://www.marseille-cassis.com/la-course/historique/) 2009-04-16 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine") (in French). Marseille-Cassis. Retrieved on 2009-10-22.

["New citizen breaks record in Chicago"](https://www.tampabay.com/archive/2000/10/23/new-citizen-breaks-record-in-chicago/). *[Tampa Bay Times](/wiki/Tampa_Bay_Times "Tampa Bay Times")*. 23 October 2000. Retrieved 22 June 2020.

Retrieved 22 June 2020.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Kenyan marathon runner (born 1973)

**Ruth Jepkoech Kutol** (born 16 May 1973) is a Kenyan long-distance and marathon runner. She won the 2000 [Venice Marathon](/wiki/Venice_Marathon "Venice Marathon"), 2003 [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid_Marathon "Madrid Marathon"), [Venice](/wiki/Venice_Marathon "Venice Marathon") and [Dublin Marathons](/wiki/Dublin_Marathon "Dublin Marathon"), 2007 [Luxembourg Marathon](/wiki/Luxembourg_Marathon "Luxembourg Marathon"), and 2010 [Tallinn Marathon](/wiki/Tallinn_Marathon "Tallinn Marathon").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ruth_Kutol&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Kutol is from [Eldoret](/wiki/Eldoret "Eldoret"), Kenya. She has six sisters and two brothers,[[1]](#cite_note-IT-1) and also has 2 daughters.[[2]](#cite_note-LA-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ruth_Kutol&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Kutol won the [Marseille-Cassis Classique Internationale](/wiki/Marseille-Cassis_Classique_Internationale "Marseille-Cassis Classique Internationale") in 1998, one second off the course record.[[3]](#cite_note-Hist-3) At the [2000 World Cross Country Championships](/wiki/2000_IAAF_World_Cross_Country_Championships "2000 IAAF World Cross Country Championships") she finished tenth in the long race, while the Kenyan team of which Kutol was a part won the silver medal in the team competition.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2000, Kutol won the [Venice Marathon](/wiki/Venice_Marathon "Venice Marathon"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) the first competitive marathon that she had run.[[2]](#cite_note-LA-2) In 2001, Kutol came second in a 5.65 km (3.51 mi) race in [San Sebastián](/wiki/San_Sebasti%C3%A1n "San Sebastián"), Spain.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In the same year, she came second in the [Paris Marathon](/wiki/Paris_Marathon "Paris Marathon"), losing in a sprint by one second to fellow Kenyan [Florence Barsosio](/wiki/Florence_Barsosio "Florence Barsosio").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 2003, Kutol won the [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid_Marathon "Madrid Marathon") and [Venice Marathons](/wiki/Venice_Marathon "Venice Marathon").[[1]](#cite_note-IT-1) Later in the year, she won the [Dublin Marathon](/wiki/Dublin_Marathon "Dublin Marathon") in a record time of 2:27.22, which was over five minutes faster than her previous best.[[1]](#cite_note-IT-1)[[8]](#cite_note-8) Her record time stood until 2010, when it was beaten by Russian [Tatyana Aryasova](/wiki/Tatyana_Aryasova "Tatyana Aryasova").[[9]](#cite_note-9) In 2004, Kutol won the [Paris Half Marathon](/wiki/Paris_Half_Marathon "Paris Half Marathon") in a time of 1:11:13.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Kutol took a two-year break from competition in order to have a baby, and returned in 2007. In that year, she won the [Luxembourg Marathon](/wiki/Luxembourg_Marathon "Luxembourg Marathon") in a time of 2:41:26.[[2]](#cite_note-LA-2)[[11]](#cite_note-11) In 2010, Kutol won the [Tallinn Marathon](/wiki/Tallinn_Marathon "Tallinn Marathon").[[12]](#cite_note-12) In 2011, she came third at the [Kuala Lumpur Marathon](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur_Marathon "Kuala Lumpur Marathon").[[13]](#cite_note-13) In 2012, she came 21st overall and second woman at the February [Bangkok Marathon](/wiki/Bangkok_Marathon "Bangkok Marathon"), which had been postponed from 2011 due to flooding.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)
